season_two_situation.your_arc: The group is no longer new to you; the question becomes what kind of Paris life you are choosing on purpose.
open_threads: Romy has a Montreal escape hatch she can no longer keep abstract.
Lila has to decide whether Berlin is a loss, a dream, or a second chance.
Gus's real family life in Creteil starts colliding with the costume.
Marin has to call his father without turning the call into an omen.
You stop practicing belonging and start deciding what you owe the people who made room for you.
season_two_situation.first_episode_seed: The morning after the season finale, Le Mistral has the same table and a very different silence.

stages.summary: A calendar alert about Romy's Montreal apartment flashes at exactly the wrong time.
Romy treats the lease decision like logistics, while everyone hears the word leaving.
Romy admits the apartment is not a backup plan; it is a fear with an address.
Berlin writes back, politely enough to be impossible to ignore.
Lila starts bargaining with a dream as if it were a difficult parent.
Lila asks the group to stop fixing her choice and witness it instead.
A Sunday errand exposes that Gus's chateau has a commuter line and a mother waiting.
Gus turns family tenderness into a performance until the performance stops protecting him.
Gus invites the group to Creteil without calling it a bit.
Marin's father leaves a voicemail that sounds ordinary only if nobody knows Marin.
Marin answers everyone except the person he actually needs to call.
Marin makes the call without asking the universe for permission first.
A practical Paris problem no longer makes you feel like a visitor.
Belonging starts costing you choices, not just courage.
You choose the life in French without treating it like an exam.
